props.title: Ethena Didn't Hold rsETH. It Fell 13% Anyway. That Gap Is What Agent-Based Simulators Are Built For.

props.text: The Kelp DAO / Aave weekend is a natural experiment for a specific claim: traditional DeFi risk models see counterparty graphs, not belief cascades. Stani said Aave's protocol wasn't compromised — $6B walked out anyway, and Ethena fell 13% with zero rsETH exposure. The article threads that gap through MiroShark's Director Mode (event injection) and today's merged Counterfactual Explorer (remove top influencers, recompute drift) — open-source attribution science for the exact postmortem Aave governance is now writing.
